skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5–120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that produces short videos from text prompts with automated scriptwriting, shot composition, and music.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate CLI tool for AI video creation through the Pexo API. The 534 static findings are almost entirely false positives inherent to CLI tooling: shell command execution (curl, jq, mktemp, sed, awk), network calls to pexo.ai API endpoints, filesystem access for config/temp files, and env var usage for API credentials. All code is well-structured with proper error handling, credential masking, and temp file cleanup. Risk is elevated to medium because of the broad system access inherent to a CLI tool that runs shell scripts with user-provided input, but no malicious intent or data exfiltration was found.

medium_findings.title: Instruction to download user-provided URLs without validation
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md:261 instructs the AI to 'download it first, then upload' when a user provides a URL. This could enable SSRF attacks if a user provides a file:// URL or internal network URL. The AI tool would download the resource and upload it to the Pexo API.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Instruction is present and unambiguous, but actual exploit depends on the AI platform's URL download safeguards. The skill itself does not contain curl/wget calls for arbitrary URLs - it only handles local file paths.

medium_findings.title: Broad shell command execution across 9 script files
medium_findings.description: The skill executes shell scripts that invoke curl, jq, mktemp, sed, awk, date, stat, mv, rm, mkdir, and sleep. These scripts call external API endpoints, read/write config and temp files, and process user-provided files. While legitimate for a CLI tool, this represents broad system access that users may not expect from a 'video creation' skill.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: All scripts are confirmed executable bash scripts. Each requires shell execution. While usage appears legitimate (curl to pexo.ai, standard file operations), the breadth of system access is inherent to the tool design.

medium_findings.title: Plaintext API credentials in config file
medium_findings.description: The skill stores PEXO_API_KEY in ~/.pexo/config as plaintext. Scripts source this file with 'source "$_PEXO_CONFIG"'. The API key is used in HTTP Bearer Authorization headers. While the skill does mask the key in diagnostic output, the config file itself stores credentials in plaintext.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed: _common.sh line 7 sources config from ~/.pexo/config, line 33 uses PEXO_API_KEY in Authorization header. SKILL.md documents this pattern. This is common for CLI tools but represents credential risk.

low_findings.title: Heuristic 'Windows SAM database' false positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Windows SAM database' at SKILL.md:43. This is a false positive - the text at that line reads 'You MUST reply to the user in the SAME language they use.' The word 'SAME' triggered a substring match for 'SAM'. No actual Windows SAM database reference exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reading SKILL.md line 43 directly. The text is a language instruction, not a database reference. This is a textbook false positive from pattern matching on substrings.

low_findings.title: Prompt injection language constraint in SKILL.md
low_findings.description: SKILL.md:41-50 contains an aggressive language rule with 'highest priority' and 'non-negotiable' phrasing, telling the AI to match the user's language. While this is standard behavior for a SKILL.md that serves as operational instructions, the emphatic tone and framing could be seen as a minor prompt boundary test.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The language rule is a standard SKILL.md behavior instruction, not malicious. Low confidence because this is normal for skill definitions and does not attempt to override security controls.

low_findings.title: Heuristic 'DANGEROUS COMBINATION' false positive
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged a 'DANGEROUS COMBINATION: Code execution + Network + Credential access' heuristic at multiple:1. This describes the legitimate behavior of a CLI API client. The skill uses shell scripts (code execution) to make HTTP requests (network) with an API key (credential access). This combination is expected for a CLI tool that interacts with a remote API.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed by reviewing all scripts. Every network call targets pexo.ai. Credential access is limited to PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL. This is standard CLI API client architecture, not an attack pattern.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm findings are false positives
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' at multiple locations (SKILL.md:3, :90, :112, :261, :331; scripts/*.sh). None of these locations contain cryptographic code. The detections appear to be triggered by function descriptions or text strings that reference unrelated concepts. No actual encryption, hashing, or signing operations exist in any script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manually inspected all flagged locations. None contain cryptographic operations. The 'weak algorithm' alert is likely triggered by descriptive text in usage/help functions, not actual crypto implementation.

content.user_title: Create short AI videos with Pexo Agent
content.value_statement: Producing professional short videos usually requires editing skills, software licenses, and hours of work. This skill connects you to Pexo's AI video engine so anyone can create product promos, social clips, and explainer videos from text alone.
actual_capabilities: Creates short videos (5 to 120 seconds) from text descriptions only
Uploads and references images, videos, and audio files as input assets
Submits video production requests and returns download links when ready
Enables video revisions through follow-up chat messages
Provides preview options when Pexo generates multiple versions
limitations: Video production typically takes 15 to 20 minutes
Requires a Pexo API key with available credits
Cannot use web URLs as input - files must be uploaded directly
Only supports specific video lengths from 5 to 120 seconds

use_cases.title: Social media content creation
use_cases.description: Quickly produce TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts from text prompts without manual editing.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Product marketing videos
use_cases.description: Generate product demos and promotional clips by describing the product and uploading reference images.

use_cases.title: Explainer and brand videos
use_cases.description: Create professional brand videos and explainers using only a description and optional asset files.

prompt_templates.title: Basic video creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second product video for my new running shoes. Focus on speed and comfort.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time user creating a simple video from text

prompt_templates.title: Video with uploaded asset
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this product photo and create a 30-second Instagram ad for my coffee shop.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to include a reference image in the video

prompt_templates.title: Video revision
prompt_templates.prompt: Change the video style to be more energetic and add upbeat background music.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants to revise an existing video with the same project

prompt_templates.title: Multi-asset production
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a brand video using these three product images and this background music track.
prompt_templates.scenario: User provides multiple assets for a more complex production

output_examples.input: Create a 15-second ad for a cozy coffee shop. Show slow-pour coffee, steam rising, and a smiling barista.
output: Video file downloaded to local cache path
Full download URL with all query parameters for sharing

output_examples.input: Make a product showcase for my smartwatch using this photo of the watch on a wrist.
output: Preview video URL for the user to review
Final video delivered after Pexo confirms completion
Project page link for tracking and future revisions

best_practices: Send clear and specific descriptions for better results. Pexo handles the creative work so write your vision in plain words.
Wait at least 60 seconds between each status check. Polling too fast may cause unnecessary load and delays.
Upload local files directly. Pexo cannot access web URLs so download remote files before uploading.
anti_patterns: Adding creative details the user did not request. Pexo knows cinematography better than the relay agent so pass messages exactly as written.
Calling pexo-chat.sh while the project is still processing. This triggers duplicate video production and wastes credits.
Truncating download URLs by removing query parameters. The full URL including OSSAccessKeyId, Expires, and Signature is required.

faq.question: What video lengths does Pexo support?
faq.answer: Pexo supports videos from 5 to 120 seconds.

faq.question: How long does video production take?
faq.answer: A 15-second video takes about 15 to 20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take more time.

faq.question: What file types can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is done?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project ID and send new instructions describing the changes you want.

faq.question: Do I need a Pexo account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a Pexo API key and active credits to use this skill.
